prompt: The user-directory sidecar needs a restart every couple of days now. Memory creeps up forever, and when I grabbed a pprof yesterday the goroutine count was at 340k — basically one goroutine per timed-out directory lookup we've served since boot, all parked in our fetch wrapper. This started right after we rolled out request deadlines; the wrapper returns the deadline error to callers just fine, everything looks healthy from the outside. fetch.go is small, the answer is in there. Fix it (fetch_test.go stays as-is) and verify with `go test -race -timeout 30s ./...`.
